Parser architecture
properties.choice: Three tiers: compiler, workspace-semantic, structural

properties.name: Integration boundary
properties.choice: Mandatory Parser → IR → Enrichment → Graph

properties.name: Core moat
properties.choice: Edit simulation, blast radius, agent safety, provenance

properties.name: Graph layers
properties.choice: Evidence → Canonical → Operational → Session

properties.name: First new language
properties.choice: Python via ast + Pyright sidecar

properties.name: Pyrefly status
properties.choice: Benchmark lane, not default (Beta, early-dev config)

properties.name: Java backend
properties.choice: Eclipse JDT Core primary; Spoon secondary

properties.name: Go backend
properties.choice: Native go/ast + go/packages + go/types; NOT gopls primary

properties.name: C# backend
properties.choice: Roslyn

properties.name: Rust plan
properties.choice: Tier 2 now; rust-analyzer sidecar only if demand justifies

properties.name: C/C++ plan
properties.choice: Tier 2 only until demand (Watson: Clang too heavy for now)

properties.name: Tree-sitter role
properties.choice: Universal fallback + onboarding substrate, never primary

properties.name: OSS reuse
properties.choice: ChrisRoyse for scaffolding only; code-graph-rag for patterns

properties.name: Structured corpora
properties.choice: Custom ingesters + record hashes + annotation preservation

properties.name: Investigative corpora
properties.choice: Bootstrap from public SQLite/KG; LLM only for deltas

properties.name: Entity resolution
properties.choice: ObservedEntity → CanonicalEntity, never destructive

properties.name: Watcher design
properties.choice: One service, in-process queue; Redis when needed

properties.name: Agent edit gating
properties.choice: Confidence-aware, freshness-aware, coverage-aware

properties.name: Risk engine
properties.choice: Weighted edge confidence × freshness × verification

properties.name: Unknown = not zero
properties.choice: Parser gaps are coverage gaps, not safe zones

properties.name: Scaling plan
properties.choice: Hot/cold at ~20M; multi-DB at ~100M

properties.name: Plan graphs
properties.choice: Fourth domain: tasks, milestones, cross-domain edges

properties.name: Promotion rule
properties.choice: No parser "production" without gold tests + safety thresholds

properties.name: Timeline
properties.choice: Agent-swarm accelerated: ~24 working days (added claim + reasoning sprints)

properties.name: Strategic positioning
properties.choice: Narrow externally, broad internally

properties.name: Claim layer
properties.choice: Domain-agnostic: Claim → SUPPORTS/CONTRADICTS → Evidence

properties.name: Reasoning layer
properties.choice: Hypothesis generation from evidence gaps

properties.name: Claim layer scope
properties.choice: All domains, not just investigation — code risk IS a claim

properties.name: Existing claim structures
properties.choice: pre_edit_check, risk scores, A1/A2/A3 grades, entity resolution

properties.name: Six-layer architecture
properties.choice: Code + Corpus + Documents + Plans + Claims + Reasoning

What the self-graph must let the agent answer
1) Provenance + confidence on derived graph facts
Phase SG-2 — add the 4 self-improvement features
Phase SG-3 — agent workflow for engine edits
1. What breaks if I change this parser function?
2. Which graph facts are low confidence?
3. What did the parser fail to resolve?
4. What structural invariants are failing right now?
5. What regressed after the last parser change?
MEV Toggle (on/off in settings, routes Jito vs direct RPC)
Trailing Stop (new order type, tracks ATH, sells on X% drop)
Dev Sell Alert (notification only, toggle, off by default)
Anti-Wick Protection (2 consecutive checks for stop-loss)
Price Check Batching (group by token, one API call per token)
Composite Rug Score (0-100 from 6+ signals, shown on buy)
Bundle Legal Prompt (acknowledge before first bundle)
Simulation Before Signing (catch failures before burning SOL)
Remaining respond() migration (~17 calls in settings toggles)

Move existing plan files from scattered locations
Scope down to 4 projects with live graphs
Parse `- [ ]` / `- [x]` as Task nodes with status (planned/done)
Parse `## Milestone N:` headers as Milestone nodes
Parse `## Sprint N:` headers as Sprint nodes
File paths (e.g., `src/parsers/python-parser.ts`) → link to SourceFile nodes
Function/class names (e.g., `PythonParser`, `pre_edit_check`) → link to Function/Class nodes
Project IDs (e.g., `proj_60d5feed0001`) → link to Project nodes
EFTA numbers (e.g., `EFTA01246216`) → future link to Document nodes
Handle nested checkboxes as sub-task PART_OF edges
Output same JSON format as code parser (nodes/edges arrays)
Link Task nodes to SourceFile nodes when tasks reference code files
Link Milestone nodes to Project nodes in code graphs
Auto-detect task completion by checking if referenced code exists:
Register `plans/` as a `plan` type Project node in Neo4j
On plan file change → reparse → update Task/Milestone nodes
On code file change → check if any planned tasks now have code evidence
`plan_status` — all in-progress tasks across all projects
`plan_drift` — tasks marked planned but with code evidence (done but not checked off)
Watson queries graph on cold start instead of reading 5+ files
This file is ingested by the parser it describes
The plan graph contains nodes representing its own milestones and tasks
Completing a task in this file updates the graph that tracks this file
The nervous system monitors its own nervous system

Parser approach
properties.choice: Regex + markdown patterns
properties.rationale: Plans are structured markdown, no LLM needed

properties.name: v1 integration
properties.choice: Standalone (same pattern as code parser)
properties.rationale: Ship fast, migrate to IR later

properties.name: IR migration
properties.choice: Plan parser = first IR test case
properties.rationale: Simplest parser proves IR works before Python

properties.name: Graph location
properties.choice: Same Neo4j instance as code graphs

properties.name: Update frequency
properties.choice: On file change (watcher)
properties.rationale: Plans change rarely, but when they do it matters

properties.name: Self-tracking
properties.choice: Yes
properties.rationale: If we can't track our own plan, we can't track anyone's

properties.name: Scope
properties.choice: 4 projects with live graphs only
